---
title: "02 Climate and weather"
author: "Alex Koiter"
---





## Load libraries





::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .hidden}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(patchwork)
  library(weathercan)
})
```
:::





## Get the data

### Find station_id and climate_id





::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .hidden}
stations_search(coords = c(49.332263, -98.382368), dist = 20, normals_years = "1991-2020")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden}

```
No stations within 20km. Returning closest 10 records
```

## Normals





::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .hidden}
df_normal_miami <-  normals_dl(climate_ids = 5021736, normals_years = "1981-2010") |>
  unnest(normals) |>
  filter(period == "Year") |>
  select(precip, rain, )
```
:::







### Download




::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .hidden}
df_normal_morden <- normals_dl(climate_ids = 5021849, normals_years = "current", measurement_type = c("Temperature", "Precipitation")) |>
  filter(period_of_record == "Normal") |>
  #filter(element_group %in% c("Precipitation", "Temperature")) |>
  select(period, precipitation_mm, rainfall_mm, daily_average_c)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden}

```
The most current normals available for download by weathercan are '1991-2020'
Using composite locations: MORDEN
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code .hidden}
morden_annual <- df_normal_morden |>
  filter(period == "Year")|>
  summarise(precipitation_mm = max(precipitation_mm, na.rm = TRUE), 
            rainfall_mm = max(rainfall_mm, na.rm = TRUE),
            daily_average_c = max(daily_average_c, na.rm = TRUE))
morden_annual
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 1 × 3
  precipitation_mm rainfall_mm daily_average_c
             <dbl>       <dbl>           <dbl>
1             528.        452.               4
```


:::
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .hidden}
morden_annual$precipitation_mm
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] 527.8
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code .hidden}
morden_month <- df_normal_morden |>
  filter(period != "Year") |>
  group_by(period) |>
  summarise(precip = max(precipitation_mm, na.rm = TRUE), 
            rainfall_mm = max(rainfall_mm, na.rm = TRUE),
            daily_c = max(daily_average_c, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  mutate(date = mdy(paste0(period, "-01-2025")))
```
:::





### Plot Climate Normals




::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .hidden}
p1 <- ggplot() +
  theme_bw() +
  geom_segment(data = morden_month, aes(x = date, y = precip/2.25 - 30, xend = date, yend = -30), linewidth = 8, colour =  "skyblue4") +
  stat_smooth(data = morden_month, aes(x = date, y = daily_c), method = "loess", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1, colour =  "black") +
  scale_y_continuous(name = expression("Temperature " ( degree*C)), 
                     sec.axis = sec_axis(~ (. + 30) * 2.25 , name = "Precipitation (mm)"),
                     limits = c(-30, 30),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +  
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b", 
               date_breaks = "1 month", 
               #expand = c(0.01,0.01), 
               name = "",
               #limits = (c(as_date("2025-01-01"), as_date("2025-12-20")))
               ) +
  annotate(geom="text", x = as_date("2025-01-15"), y = 25, label = "1991-2020 Climate Normals - Morden",
           color="black", hjust = 0) +
  annotate(geom="text", x = as_date("2025-01-15"), y = 20, label = "Annual Temp = 4.0 \u00B0C",
           color="black", hjust = 0) +
  annotate(geom="text", x = as_date("2025-01-15"), y = 15, label = "Annual Precip = 527.8 mm",
           color="black", hjust = 0)
p1
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden}

```
`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
```


:::

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](weather_discharge_files/figure-jats/unnamed-chunk-8-1.png)
:::
:::






## Study period weather

### Download





::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .hidden}
df_day <- weather_dl(station_ids = 29886, interval = "day", start = "2021-10-01", end = "2023-09-30") |>
  select(date, mean_temp, total_precip) |>
  mutate(water_year = case_when(
    month(date) >= 10 ~ year(date) + 1,
    TRUE ~ year(date)
  )) |>
  group_by(water_year) %>%
  mutate(cumul = cumsum(replace_na(total_precip, 0))) %>%
  ungroup()
```
:::





### Sampling details




::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .hidden}
vline_data <- data.frame(
  water_year = c(2022, 2022, 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023),
  x = as.Date(c("2022-05-24", "2022-05-25", "2022-10-29", "2023-04-01", "2022-10-21", "2023-05-11", "2023-09-19", "2023-05-11"))
)

annotation_data <- data.frame(
  water_year = c(2022, 2022, 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023, 2023),
  x = as.Date(c("2022-05-24", "2022-05-25", "2022-10-29", "2023-04-01", "2022-10-21", "2023-05-11", "2023-09-19", "2023-05-11")), 
  y = c(35, 0, 35, 35, 35, 35, 35, 0),
  label = c("ESN", "Seeding", "Tillage", "Runoff", "Soil", "Soil", "Soil", "Seeding")
) |>
  mutate(x = case_when(label %in% c("Seeding", "Tillage") ~ x + 11,
                       TRUE ~ x))
```
:::





### Plot study weather




::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .hidden}
p2 <- ggplot() +
  theme_bw() +
  geom_point(data = df_day, aes(x = date, y = mean_temp, color = ifelse(mean_temp > 0, "#dc2626", "#2563eb"))) +
  scale_color_identity() +
  geom_line(data = df_day, aes(x = date, y = cumul/8 - 35), colour =  "skyblue4", linewidth = 1) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = expression("Temperature " ( degree*C)), 
                     sec.axis = sec_axis(~ (. + 35) * 8 , name = "Precipitation (mm)"),
                     #limits = c(-30, 30),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b", date_breaks = "1 month") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5)) +
  facet_wrap(~water_year, scales = "free_x", ncol=1) +
geom_vline(data = vline_data,
             aes(xintercept = x),
             color = "black", 
             linewidth = 0.5) +
 geom_text(data = annotation_data,
            aes(x = x, y = y, label = label),
            color = "black",
            angle = 90,
            hjust = 1,
            vjust = -0.25,
            inherit.aes = FALSE)
p2
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden}

```
Warning: Removed 6 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
(`geom_point()`).
```


:::

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](weather_discharge_files/figure-jats/unnamed-chunk-11-1.png)
:::
:::





## Combined plot





::: {#cell-fig-weather-climate .cell}

```{.r .cell-code .hidden}
#| label: fig-weather-climate
#| fig-height: 10
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| fig-cap: "a) Climate Normals (1991-2020) for Morden, MB (Climate ID 5021849), located approximately 30 km from the field site. b) Weather conditions for the 2022 and 2023 water years (Oct 1 - Sept 30) for Deerwood, MB (Station ID 29886), located approximately 8 km from the field site. Vertical lines indicate the timing of (1) application of Environmental Smart Nitrogen (ESN) (polymer-coated slow-release urea); (2) seeding and tillage field operations; and (3) collection of soil and runoff samples. Red and blue points indicate average daily temperatures above and below 0 degrees Celsius, respectively."

p1 / p2 + 
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 2), axis_title = "collect") +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a", tag_suffix = ")")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden}

```
`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
```


:::

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr .hidden}

```
Warning: Removed 6 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
(`geom_point()`).
```


:::

::: {.cell-output-display}
![a) Climate Normals (1991-2020) for Morden, MB (Climate ID 5021849), located approximately 30 km from the field site. b) Weather conditions for the 2022 and 2023 water years (Oct 1 - Sept 30) for Deerwood, MB (Station ID 29886), located approximately 8 km from the field site. Vertical lines indicate the timing of (1) application of Environmental Smart Nitrogen (ESN) (polymer-coated slow-release urea); (2) seeding and tillage field operations; and (3) collection of soil and runoff samples. Red and blue points indicate average daily temperatures above and below 0 degrees Celsius, respectively.](weather_discharge_files/figure-jats/fig-weather-climate-1.png){#fig-weather-climate}
:::
